mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and liquidity conditions to equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is falling rather than flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after non-declines, or if the sign of the effect is negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still rising, the market discounts further tightening and multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative median 20d SPX returns, or if returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered cases over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand expectations, and a monthly increase in unemployment should precede weaker S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 20d SPX returns are not below unconditional average, or if the signal's hit rate for negative returns falls below 50% in any 24-month rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE confirms broad labor-market strengthening, which supports consumer spending and earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines fail to outperform baseline 60d returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the interaction term is not needed.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in RSAFS signals stronger retail spending, implying better near-term revenue momentum for cyclicals and the broader index, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months do not produce higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign reverses in periods when retail sales are accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO together with stable CPIAUCSL indicates improving real output without renewed inflation pressure, which supports margins and should raise S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not beat either proxy alone in out-of-sample directional accuracy, or if triggered returns are not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing-demand weakness and tighter wealth effects, which should pressure cyclical earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large housing contractions are not followed by below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when housing weakness persists for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a conjunction of high FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL implies real-rate pressure and multiple compression, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening/inflation condition does not predict negative 5d SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered observations.
